parsed.mechanism: Off-fault structural damage induces high-frequency wave radiation and reduces rupture speed; transferring this principle, a SimBal-style auxiliary loss that softly encourages orthogonality in MoE router weights produces controlled 'damage' to the routing manifold that regulates token-routing rupture dynamics and routes load away from over-concentrated experts.
parsed.transfer: The orthogonality-inducing auxiliary loss mechanism from A transfers to B as a tunable perturbation that regulates the speed and energy distribution of a propagating routing/rupture front.
parsed.open_problem: Can an orthogonality-based auxiliary loss be calibrated to controllably damp high-frequency instability and slow over-fast convergence in MoE routing, analogous to how off-fault damage reduces rupture speed?
parsed.niche_name: Damage-Regularized Orthogonal Routing for Stable MoE Load Balancing
parsed.primary_quote: softly encouraging orthogonality in the router weights
